title: Deep Learning: Build Real Things

modules.title: What Deep Learning Actually Is (Skip the Hype)
modules.sub: Separating real capabilities from buzzwords and marketing noise
modules.description: Learners get a clear, grounded definition of deep learning and where it fits within AI and machine learning broadly. By the end, you can confidently explain what deep learning does and doesn't do, and why it matters right now.

modules.title: How Neural Networks Learn: The Intuition Behind the Math
modules.sub: Understanding weights, layers, and gradients without drowning in equations
modules.description: You build a mental model of how a neural network adjusts itself through training, tracing the journey from input to prediction to error correction. The focus is on intuition first ‚Äî you leave knowing what backpropagation and gradient descent actually mean, not just that they exist.

modules.title: Your First Model: Hands-On in 30 Minutes
modules.sub: Writing and running real deep learning code for the first time
modules.description: You open a free cloud notebook and train a working neural network on a classic dataset, following along line by line. By the end of the module you have a model that makes real predictions ‚Äî and a mental checkpoint for what 'building with AI' actually feels like.

modules.title: Computer Vision √¢¬Ä¬î Teaching Machines to See
modules.sub: How AI recognizes images, faces, objects, and scenes
modules.description: You explore how convolutional neural networks process visual information and power tools like photo tagging, medical imaging, and self-driving cars. You experiment with a pre-trained vision model to classify images and start to understand what the network is actually 'looking at.'

modules.title: Natural Language Processing √¢¬Ä¬î Teaching Machines to Read
modules.sub: How AI understands, generates, and reasons about human language
modules.description: You dig into how models like GPT and BERT turn words into numbers and find patterns in text. You run hands-on experiments with a language model to see tokenization, embeddings, and predictions in action ‚Äî making abstract NLP concepts concrete.

modules.title: Training, Testing, and Knowing When Your Model Fails
modules.sub: Evaluating model performance and diagnosing what goes wrong
modules.description: You learn how to split data properly, read evaluation metrics, and spot the warning signs of overfitting and underfitting. This module shifts your mindset from 'did it run?' to 'is it actually working?' ‚Äî a critical skill for building models you can trust.

modules.title: Tools of the Trade: PyTorch, Hugging Face, and Free GPUs
modules.sub: Navigating the real ecosystem builders use to get things done
modules.description: You get a practical tour of the tools professionals rely on ‚Äî PyTorch for building models, Hugging Face for pre-trained models and datasets, and free GPU platforms like Google Colab and Kaggle. You set up your own working environment so you're ready to build independently.

modules.title: Build Something Real: Your Own Mini Deep Learning Project
modules.sub: Choosing a problem, training a model, and shipping something yours
modules.description: You pick a project idea, work through the full pipeline from data to trained model to evaluation, and present what you built and what you learned. This capstone turns everything in the course into a real artifact ‚Äî something you made, understand, and can build on.

synopsis: Deep learning is what powers the AI tools everyone is talking about √¢¬Ä¬î and you can start building with it without a PhD or expensive hardware. This course gives you the conceptual foundation and hands-on experience to go from curious to capable.
rationale: As AI becomes infrastructure, young builders who understand deep learning fundamentals √¢¬Ä¬î not just how to prompt tools √¢¬Ä¬î will have a massive advantage in technical careers and entrepreneurship.
learning_outcome: After this course, you can train and evaluate a basic deep learning model, understand how major AI systems work under the hood, and know where to go next to specialize.
source_signals: Tracing the thoughts of a large language model
Machine Learning 101 slidedeck: 2 years of headbanging, so you don't have to
